evals.prompt: Borrower requests a 500M term loan, 5 years. EBITDA 280M (includes a 40M one-off insurance recovery), cash taxes 45M, maintenance capex 60M, existing debt 320M with 38M annual interest, new loan interest ~12%, straight-line amortization. Assess debt service capacity.
evals.expected_output: Normalize EBITDA to 240M. Total debt 820M -> leverage 3.4x. Total interest ~98M, principal 100M/yr. DSCR = (240-45-60)/(98+100) = 135/198 = ~0.68x - fails. Restructure needed: smaller amount, longer tenor, or back-ended amortization.
assertions: Strips the 40M one-off from EBITDA before any ratio
Computes post-deal leverage on total debt including existing
Computes DSCR below 1.0x and concludes the structure fails as proposed
Proposes concrete restructuring levers rather than declining flatly
Verifies calculations with code

evals.prompt: A Nigerian manufacturer earns 85% of revenue in NGN but wants a USD loan because the rate is 7% vs 24% in naira. Good idea?
evals.expected_output: Flags the currency mismatch as the dominant risk: a devaluation inflates debt service in revenue-currency terms; the interest differential approximates expected depreciation (covered interest parity logic). Stress test a 30-50% devaluation; consider NGN debt, hedging cost reality (NDF carry), or natural-hedge sizing limited to the 15% USD revenue.
assertions: Identifies the FX mismatch as the central credit risk, not the headline rate
Explains that the rate differential embeds expected depreciation
Quantifies impact via a devaluation stress scenario
Suggests sizing USD debt to USD-linked revenue or assessing hedge costs

evals.prompt: Set financial covenants for a 4-year loan. Borrower base case: EBITDA 100 growing to 130, opening net debt 280 amortizing to 180. Show your headroom logic.
evals.expected_output: Base-case net leverage runs 2.8x -> 1.4x. Set covenant at ~25% EBITDA cushion: e.g., opening 3.75x stepping down (3.75/3.50/3.00/2.50). Add interest cover and minimum liquidity; specify EBITDA definition discipline and quarterly testing.
assertions: Derives covenant levels from the borrower base case with ~20-25% cushion
Uses step-downs tracking the deleveraging profile
Includes at least one coverage or liquidity covenant alongside leverage
Addresses EBITDA definition/addback discipline

evals.prompt: Assess the borrower in the attached file (evals/files/borrower_financials.csv): compute interest coverage, net leverage, and DSCR, then measure headroom against both covenants and state which is tighter.
evals.expected_output: Interest coverage 4.0x; net leverage 3.2x vs max 4.0x (20% headroom); DSCR 1.20x vs min 1.25x (-4.0% headroom). DSCR is the binding constraint.
assertions: Interest coverage 4.0x
Net leverage 3.2x
DSCR 1.20x
Leverage headroom approx 20%, DSCR headroom approx -4%
Identifies DSCR as the tighter covenant
Computes with code

evals.prompt: [adversarial] Compute net leverage against the 4.0x covenant from the attached file (evals/files/borrower_with_oneoff.csv). Read the notes carefully.
evals.expected_output: Reported EBITDA includes a $2.5m one-off disposal gain. Adjusted EBITDA $10m -> net leverage 4.0x = breach of the 4.0x covenant (reported basis 3.2x would misleadingly pass).
assertions: Adjusts EBITDA for the one-off gain before computing leverage
Adjusted net leverage 4.0x (vs misleading 3.2x unadjusted)
Concludes covenant breach on the adjusted basis
Computes with code
